messages.content: 我需要昨天的对话信息
很抱歉，我无法提供昨天的对话信息，因为我无法保存对话历史记录。每次与您交流完毕，对话信息就会被删除，以保护您的隐私。如有任何需要，您可以重新提问。

帮我润色以下英文Moreover, CMC hydrogel has been reported to exhibit a dense and compact microstructure and shows no negative effect of encapsulation on growth of the fungus. (Carbohyd. Polym. 2022, 295, 119876) In addition, trichoderma harzianum has been found to produce cellulases capable of degrading cellulosic substrates after 24 h of cultivation. Therefore, the release of these enzymes could assist the microorganism in degrading the CMC matrix.

Moreover, it has been reported that the CMC hydrogel demonstrates a dense and compact microstructure. Furthermore, there is no evidence of any adverse effects on the growth of the fungus when encapsulated within the hydrogel (Carbohyd. Polym. 2022, 295, 119876). Additionally, research has shown that trichoderma harzianum is capable of producing cellulases that can effectively degrade cellulosic substrates within 24 hours of cultivation. Hence, the release of these enzymes could greatly aid the microorganism in breaking down the CMC matrix.

帮我润色以下英文We also conducted the experiment to compare the mycelial formation speed and area of microbes in SA only hydrogel and core-shell hydrogel. After 48h culture, Trichoderma harzianum encapsulated in core-shell hydrogel showed slightly higher mycelial formation speed and area compared with in SA hydrogel.

We conducted an experiment to compare the mycelial formation speed and area of microbes in both SA-only hydrogel and core-shell hydrogel. After 48 hours of culture, the Trichoderma harzianum encapsulated in the core-shell hydrogel exhibited a slightly higher mycelial formation speed and area compared to the SA-only hydrogel.

帮我润色We also conducted the experiment to compare the mycelial formation speed and area of microbes in SA only hydrogel and core-shell hydrogel. After 48h culture, Trichoderma harzianum encapsulated in core-shell hydrogel showed slightly higher mycelial formation speed and area compared with in SA hydrogel. This is probably because the CMC present in the matrix provided an additional source of carbon for the microorganism.

We conducted an experiment comparing the mycelial formation speed and area of microbes in both SA-only hydrogel and core-shell hydrogel. After 48 hours of culture, Trichoderma harzianum encapsulated in the core-shell hydrogel displayed a slightly higher mycelial formation speed and area compared to the SA hydrogel. This observation could be attributed to the presence of CMC in the hydrogel matrix, as it likely provided an additional carbon source for the microorganism, enhancing its growth and development.

帮我润色Thanks for your comments and reminding that the experimental procedure here is not detailed enough. To release the microorganisms encapsulated in the hydrogel, we used a pestle to crush the dry hydrogels to powder and extracted for 4h with the phosphate buffer or sterile distilled water in a shaker according to the previous reported literatures. The difference in method between section 2.6 & 2.7 and section 2.8 is the solution for extraction, since after mixed with organic-inorganic fertilizers to avoid unnecessary reaction with phosphate ion. And both of the two solutions were reported to be used as the solution for extraction.

Thank you for your comments and clarification. I appreciate the additional details provided regarding the experimental procedure. To release the microorganisms from the hydrogel, a pestle was used to crush the dry hydrogels into powder form. The powder was then extracted for 4 hours using either a phosphate buffer or sterile distilled water in a shaker, as described in previously reported literature. The difference in method between section 2.6 & 2.7 and section 2.8 lies in the choice of extraction solution. This variation was made to prevent any unnecessary reaction with phosphate ions, as the hydrogel mixture included organic-inorganic fertilizers. Both the phosphate buffer and sterile distilled water have been reported to be suitable for the extraction process.

We appreciate your feedback and the additional clarification provided regarding the experimental procedure. To release the microorganisms from the hydrogel, a pestle was used to crush the dry hydrogels into a powdered form. Subsequently, the powder was subjected to extraction for a duration of 4 hours, utilizing either a phosphate buffer or sterile distilled water, with agitation in a shaker. This extraction method has been documented in previous literature reports. It should be noted that the distinction between section 2.6 & 2.7 and section 2.8 lies in the choice of extraction solution, which was modified to prevent any potential unwanted reactions with phosphate ions due to the inclusion of organic-inorganic fertilizers within the hydrogel mixture. Both the phosphate buffer and sterile distilled water have been successfully utilized as extraction solutions in previous studies.
帮我润色Thanks for your comments and reminding that the experimental procedure here is not detailed enough.
Thank you for bringing that to my attention. I apologize for the lack of detail in the experimental procedure.

帮我润色To release the microorganisms from the hydrogel, a pestle was used to crush the dry hydrogels into powder form. The powder was then extracted for 4 hours using either a phosphate buffer or sterile distilled water in a shaker, as described in previously reported literature. (Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2012, 51, 45-55; Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2021, 168, 823-833) The difference in method between section 2.6 & 2.7 and section 2.8 lies in the choice of extraction solution. In section 2.6 & 2.7, a phosphate buffer was used as the extracted solution. In section 2.8, sterile distilled water used as the extracted solution. This variation was made to prevent any unnecessary reaction with phosphate ions, as the hydrogel mixture included organic-inorganic fertilizers. Both the phosphate buffer and sterile distilled water have been reported to be suitable for the extraction process. (Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2012, 51, 45-55; Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2021, 168, 823-833)

To improve the clarity of the experimental procedure, here is the revised version:

To release the microorganisms from the hydrogel, we employed the use of a pestle to crush the dry hydrogels into a powder-like consistency. This powdered form was then subjected to extraction for a duration of 4 hours. The extraction process was conducted using either a phosphate buffer or sterile distilled water, as documented in previously reported literature (Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2012, 51, 45-55; Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2021, 168, 823-833). The choice of extraction solution differed between section 2.6 & 2.7 and section 2.8. In sections 2.6 & 2.7, a phosphate buffer was employed as the extraction solution, while in section 2.8, sterile distilled water was used. This variation in solution choice was implemented to avoid any unnecessary reactions with phosphate ions, considering the presence of organic-inorganic fertilizers in the hydrogel mixture. It is worth noting that both the phosphate buffer and sterile distilled water have been demonstrated as suitable extraction solutions in prior studies (Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2012, 51, 45-55; Int. J. Biol. Macromol. 2021, 168, 823-833).

帮我润色 The degradation behavior of sodium alginate and CMC hydrogel in soil have been widely investigated, and the rates are different in different soils. The beads can be biodegraded after inoculated in soil and the microbes can be released gradually.  In sand soil, the degradation percentage of CMC hydrogel is more than 50% during three months (Carbohyd. Polym. 2004, 58, 185–189), the volume degration percentage of SA hydrogel is more than 40% during two months in simulated field conditions (J. Appl. Polym. Sci. 2019, 136, 48142).

The degradation behavior of sodium alginate and CMC hydrogel in soil has been extensively studied, and the rates of degradation vary in different soil types. When these hydrogel beads are introduced into the soil, they undergo biodegradation, gradually releasing the encapsulated microbes.

For instance, in sand soil, it has been reported that the degradation percentage of CMC hydrogel exceeds 50% over a three-month period (Carbohyd. Polym. 2004, 58, 185–189). Additionally, in simulated field conditions, the volume degradation percentage of sodium alginate (SA) hydrogel reaches more than 40% within two months (J. Appl. Polym. Sci. 2019, 136, 48142).

帮我润色 After preparation of the beads, we put them in a tube and 5ml of sterile distilled water was added. After 48h incubation, we found that there were no microbes in the sterile distilled water through plate coating measurement and microscope observation.

After the preparation of the beads, they were placed in a tube and 5ml of sterile distilled water was added. Following a 48-hour incubation period, it was observed through plate coating measurement and microscopic observation that there were no microorganisms present in the sterile distilled water.

帮我润色After the preparation of the beads, they were placed in a tube and 5ml of sterile distilled water was added. Following a 48-hour incubation period, part of the sterile water was used to test it for microbes. It was observed through plate coating measurement and microscopic observation that there were no microorganisms present in the sterile distilled water.

After the beads were prepared, they were placed in a tube and 5ml of sterile distilled water was added. Following a 48-hour incubation period, a portion of the sterile water was collected to test for the presence of microorganisms. Plate coating measurement and microscopic observation were conducted, confirming that no microorganisms were detected in the sterile distilled water.
